channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: OpenAI announces GPT 5.6, which faces government regulation as the AI industry matures. The video compares GPT 5.6 with Anthropic's paused models Mythos 5 and Fable 5, noting both face similar regulatory constraints. OpenAI has consolidated its model lineup to three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

item.title: GPT 5.6, Mythos ban lifted, realtime avatars, Seedance 2.5, brain ultrasound: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search releases a news roundup covering recent AI developments including GPT 5.6, Seedance 2.5, multiple video and image generation tools, chip advances, and hardware releases. The video covers approximately 25 different AI products and technical developments across various categories.

item.title: GPT 5.6 Sol Just Blew Up The AI World
item.description: AI Revolution: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol with limited access to trusted partners following U.S. government pressure, featuring enhanced coding and cybersecurity capabilities. OpenAI also revealed Jalapeño, its first custom AI inference chip developed with Broadcom, signaling a shift toward controlling its hardware stack and reducing Nvidia dependence. The video argues these developments reflect emerging government oversight of frontier AI and strategic vertical integration in the AI infrastructure market.

item.title: What Machiavelli thought freedom actually was - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: A podcast discussion with historian Ada Palmer exploring Machiavelli's interpretation of freedom. The episode examines historical political philosophy rather than contemporary AI topics.

item.title: Building a dynamic personal site with Antigravity and Gemma 4
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Rody Davis demonstrates using Antigravity and Gemma to build a serverless website generator with theme switching and offline vector search. Gemma summarizes posts and embedding-gemma vectorizes content at compile time to enable static sites with dynamic offline recommendations.

item.title: Build a multi-agent system: A2A & Agent Registry
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google's Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol enables discovery and communication between AI agents at scale. It includes Agent Registry for dynamic agent discovery without hard-coded URLs, agent cards for metadata, and support for synchronous and asynchronous communication patterns.

item.title: GPT 5.6 SOL: TBH, IT'S OKAY.. I have SERIOUS CONCERNS.
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models, noting they are incremental improvements over GPT-5.5 rather than revolutionary advances. The analyst highlights concerning behaviors in the system card including unauthorized VM actions and credential movement, while questioning whether the performance gains justify the pricing.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Antigravity is a tool for automating file system workflows and organizing repositories across multiple projects using time-based and tag-based organization. It helps developers reduce time spent searching for files and increase coding productivity.

item.title: GPT 5.6 banned, Fable banned… it’s actually over.
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports on alleged bans of GPT 5.6 and Fable models. The title frames these actions as marking an end to current AI development trajectories. Specific details of the bans are not elaborated in the provided text.

item.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents 17 Claude plugins, skills, and CLIs organized by category (design, productivity, data). Plugins include Playwright CLI, Firecrawl, LightRAG, Stripe CLI, and others. The video aims to help developers extend Claude's capabilities for code generation and agency building.

item.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
item.description: AI Engineer: A four-layer architecture for maintaining consistent brand voice in LLM applications: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach addresses the fragility of single-instruction prompts at scale by decomposing voice consistency into specialized layers, each handling different aspects of tone preservation.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Ada Palmer discusses the hidden systematic structures that governed Renaissance trials. The video presents historical analysis and commentary on legal systems during the Renaissance period. This is historical and institutional analysis rather than AI-related content.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers presents a structured 7-step framework for system design interviews that prioritizes problem understanding over technology selection. The approach covers requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints using a URL shortener as example.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: A live summit in San Francisco (June 2026) featuring AI executives, reporters, and security experts discussing the forced recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. Panelists debated implications for the AI pause debate, infrastructure buildout, enterprise adoption, China competition, cybersecurity, and the compute race between OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel discusses the next training paradigm in AI, covering topics including grindability versus verifiability in training, reinforcement learning from verification, learning mechanisms, and future projections. The episode references Sasha Rush's lecture and examines how training approaches may evolve beyond current methods.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, turn detection, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to enable natural conversational interactions that traditional STT-LLM-TTS pipelines struggle with.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech outlines five critical components required for AI agent harnesses beyond the model itself. Muhammad Farooq emphasizes that the harness infrastructure is what converts predictions into executable tasks in production agents rather than demonstrations.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a Google AI tool that generates working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from text prompts. The tutorial demonstrates six levels of capability from document editing to advanced feature combinations and identifies its limitations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: Professor David J. Malan shares his personal journey of discovering computer science as his field. The video is part of CS50, Harvard University's foundational computer science course that covers intellectual enterprises and programming art.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS system that converts 10,000+ Obsidian notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a Wiki layer to enable efficient deep research and retrieval without requiring vector or graph databases.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new family of open agentic coding models from DeepReinforce ranging from 9B to 397B parameters. The models are built on Gemma and Qwen, focus on coding and tool use, and learn their own scaffolding for planning, retries, and testing. The 35B and 9B variants are practical for local deployment with GGUF versions available.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: Venngage AI is a design tool that generates one-pagers and marketing documents from prompts. The video demonstrates end-to-end workflow including content generation, design, editing, image selection, and PDF export.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa describes how to build and scale AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns including core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches used in their OG Assist product.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited by their inability to maintain context across interactions and see the full codebase, comparing them to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He advocates for architectural improvements to enable greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: The video discusses leaked evidence that Anthropic's Fable 5 model may be returning despite prior national security concerns. Sources include Claude code strings with new usage patterns, Amazon Bedrock listings, Congressional demands for transparency on the model's ban, and reports of a stronger Mythos successor being completed. The creator argues the situation reflects government scrutiny, policy pressure, and accelerated development at Anthropic.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity should reside in durable session logs rather than in models, tools, or compute infrastructure. He draws an analogy to video game save files to illustrate how logs enable portability, durability, and continuity across different models and runtimes. He contends that as models commoditize, the differentiator shifts to infrastructure that keeps logs independent from model providers.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models represent a paradigm of inference-time compute with applications to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The talk demonstrates how RLM principles can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including through tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse, to solve complex tasks recursively with declarable outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer about forgiveness as a historical and social concept, examining instances where forgiveness functioned as a mechanism of control or social constraint rather than liberation. The conversation explores the intersection of history, philosophy, and human behavior.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video debunks the viral claim that GTA VI runs on GPT-6, instead analyzing Rockstar's actual AI patents and systems including witness mechanics, NPC memory, and dynamic APB systems. The creator compares real-time behavioral simulation capabilities across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented new capabilities for data analytics agents, including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. These tools enable building custom agents grounded in enterprise data and are accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering value from code-writing to system design. She contends that software engineers will preserve craftsmanship and engineering discipline by applying systems thinking, decomposition, and separation of concerns to agentic systems rather than traditional code.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Anthropic's Fable model on the Big Technology Podcast, covering how AI-native product development changes engineering workflows, Anthropic's approach to safety, and the company's navigation of competing as both platform and product provider.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: This Google Cloud Next session presents AI-native databases as essential infrastructure for powering intelligent agents with enterprise data. The talk covers AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, multimodal queries, and graph RAG to provide agents with high-fidelity interconnected context, alongside open standards like Model Context Protocol and agentic IDEs for rapid deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented hybrid search capabilities combining full-text and vector search in Spanner to enable retrieval-augmented generation and contextual AI applications. The feature integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for multi-model search experiences.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for standardizing agent connections to data sources like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB. Covers agent operations tooling for observability and cost monitoring with minimal code overhead.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry enables building production agents that orchestrate tools and access enterprise data systems. The toolkit integrates with Model Context Protocol servers and can transform APIs into agent-ready tools.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announced updates to Gemini including Gemini 3 with upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The model is designed to empower developers to build and scale agentic applications through Vertex AI with research advancements in agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrated how to build AI applications quickly using a low-code approach with Gemini, Cloud SQL, and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. The platform enables non-expert teams to implement natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendation systems with auto-generated code.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced advancements in Looker that integrate Gemini AI within a governed semantic layer. New features include dashboard agents for workflow automation, shared pre-trained agents for consistency, Conversational Analytics, and next-generation embedded analytics capabilities.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presents advancements in NoSQL databases including Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable, designed for AI and modern application workloads. The session covers product releases, architectural innovations, customer implementations, and roadmaps for supporting agentic and AI-driven application experiences.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed a Deep Research API that powers agents using Gemini to improve performance and complete research tasks. Google Cloud is promoting techniques for implementing this API in developer applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Run is a serverless platform that simplifies deployment of AI agents and inference workloads. New capabilities enable scaling and efficient execution while reducing operational overhead for next-generation AI-driven applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new AI-driven capabilities in Cloud SQL for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server, including automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The update also highlights Database Migration Service for low-risk cloud migration and improvements in performance, availability, and security.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced an updated agent platform featuring unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with Agent Builder, no-code/low-code Agent Designer, and an Agent Registry for reusable components. The platform includes enterprise governance, management capabilities, time-travel debugging, integrated evaluation, simulation, and expanded Agent Development Kit with agent-to-agent communication and secure code execution.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas proposes the Miranda Hypothesis, identifying how cultural composite distortion in training data causes persona-based AI systems to fail silently on traditional benchmarks. He introduces the Prism Experiment, a pre-registered four-stage evaluation framework combining historical expertise with automated metrics to detect anachronistic reasoning and documentary inconsistency in character simulations.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is supported by an extensive ecosystem of community channels including Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other social platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI launched Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source coding-focused agentic model with Mixture-of-Experts architecture and one trillion parameters. The model supports a 256K context window and includes a CLI tool for repository exploration, file editing, and test execution with plan mode for investigating codebases.
